title: Niobe in Distress by Phillis Wheatley
content:
Apollo's wrath to man the dreadful spring
Of ills innum'rous, tuneful goddess, sing!
Thou who did'st first th' ideal pencil give,
And taught'st the pa

title: An Hymn To Humanity (To S.P.G. Esp) by Phillis Wheatley
content:
O! for this dark terrestrial ball
Forsakes his azure-paved hall
    A prince of heav'nly birth!
Divine Humanity behold,
What wonders rise, what charms

title: To The Honourable T. H. Esq; On the Death Of His Daughter by Phillis Wheatley
content:
WHILE deep you mourn beneath the cypress-shade
The hand of Death, and your dear daughter
laid

title: A Funeral Poem on the Death of C.E. by Phillis Wheatley
content:
Through airy roads he wings his instant flight
To purer regions of celestial light;
Enlarg'd he sees unnumber'd systems roll,
Beneath him sees the uni

title: To a Gentleman and Lady on the Death of the Lady's Brother and Sister by Phillis Wheatley
content:
On Death's domain intent I fix my eyes,
Where human nature in vast ruin lies,
With pensive mind I search the drear abode,
